data.title: Market Chatter: Telefonica Hires JPMorgan for Sale of Mexican Operations
data.description:
Telefonica has hired JPMorgan to facilitate the sale of its Mexican operations, local media outlet Cinco Dias reported Friday, citing unnamed financial sources.

The company aims to complete the sale before its annual shareholders meeting in April or May, according to the report.

Telefonica received interest from five potential buyers for its Argentine business and wants to close it "as soon as possible", presumably before summer, local media outlet El Economista reported earlier this week.

Telefonica is also exploring to sell its majority stake in its Colombian unit to New York-listed Millicom, Reuters reported Friday.

JPMorgan declined to comment when asked by MT Newswires. Telefonica did not immediately respond to requests for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: JETBLUE AIRWAYS CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
JetBlue Airways Corp, a major American low-cost airline, has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of JetBlue's efforts to navigate a competitive industry landscape while focusing on enhancing customer experience and operational efficiency.

Financial Highlights

JetBlue Airways Corp reported the following key financial metrics for 2024:

  • Total Operating Revenues: $9,279 million, reflecting a decrease of 3.5% compared to 2023, primarily due to lower capacity.
  • Operating Loss: $684 million, compared to an operating loss of $230 million in 2023, with an operating margin of (7.4)% for 2024.
  • Net Loss: $795 million, compared to a net loss of $310 million in 2023, primarily due to the write-off of Spirit-related costs.
  • Loss Per Common Share: $2.30, compared to a loss per share of $0.93 in 2023.

Business Highlights

JetBlue's business performance in 2024 included several key operational highlights:

  • JetBlue Experience: The airline offers a distinctive flying experience with three main options: the core experience, EvenMore®, and Mint®. The core experience includes four fare options, all providing free carry-on bags, seatback entertainment, high-speed Wi-Fi, snacks, and non-alcoholic beverages. EvenMore® offers additional legroom, priority security access, and early boarding, while Mint® provides a premium service with lie-flat beds and exclusive amenities.
  • Network and Geographical Performance: JetBlue operates a predominantly point-to-point system with 96% of routes touching one of six focus cities: New York, Boston, Fort Lauderdale-Hollywood, Orlando, Los Angeles, and San Juan. As of December 31, 2024, JetBlue served 105 destinations across 28 states, the District of Columbia, Puerto Rico, the U.S. Virgin Islands, and 31 countries in the Caribbean, Latin America, Canada, and Europe.
  • Product and Service Enhancements: In 2024, JetBlue announced plans to launch a domestic first-class experience across its non-Mint® fleet and to open airport lounges at JFK and Boston Logan International Airports. The EvenMore® Space was rebranded to EvenMore®, offering additional perks such as dedicated overhead bin space and premium snacks.
  • Operational Metrics: JetBlue's on-time performance improved to 74.1% in 2024 from 67.4% in 2023, with a completion factor increase to 98.6% from 98.1%. The airline was ranked 6th in the Wall Street Journal's 2024 Airline Rankings, a three-spot improvement from the previous year.
  • Sustainability Initiatives: JetBlue is focused on decarbonizing operations, with a goal to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 50% per revenue tonne kilometer by 2035. The company has committed to converting 40% of its Ground Service Equipment to electric power by 2025 and 50% by 2030, and aims to convert 10% of its jet fuel usage to blended Sustainable Aviation Fuel by 2030.
  • Fleet and Maintenance: As of December 31, 2024, JetBlue had an operating fleet of 290 aircraft. The company is addressing engine inspection requirements for certain Airbus A220 and A321neo aircraft, with 11 aircraft grounded due to engine availability issues.
  • Future Outlook: JetBlue plans to continue expanding its network in high-value geographies, investing in industry-leading products, and providing award-winning service. The company is also focused on maintaining a healthy liquidity balance and managing its cost structure to support long-term profitability.

Strategic Initiatives

JetBlue's strategic initiatives and capital management activities in 2024 included:

  • JetForward: JetBlue announced JetForward, a strategic framework focusing on delivering reliable service, building a strong east coast leisure network, offering valued products and perks, and ensuring a secure financial future. The company redeployed 20% of its network to focus on core geographies and introduced new customer experience enhancements, including preferred seating and a premium co-branded credit card.
  • Capital Management: JetBlue deferred approximately $3.0 billion in capital expenditures related to Airbus aircraft deliveries, shifting these to 2030 and beyond. The company raised $2.8 billion through TrueBlue Financings, issued $460 million in convertible senior notes, and completed $668 million in failed sale-leaseback transactions. Additionally, JetBlue repaid $748 million on outstanding debt and finance lease obligations.
  • Future Outlook: JetBlue plans to continue its capital-light growth strategy by extending the lives of certain A320 aircraft and maintaining a strong liquidity position. The company expects to meet its long-term liquidity needs through projected cash from operations, available lines of credit, and debt financing. JetBlue is also focused on achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2040, supported by tax credits and sustainable aviation fuel initiatives.

Challenges and Risks

JetBlue faces several challenges and risks, as outlined in its 2024 10-K report:

  • Competitive Risks: JetBlue operates in an extremely competitive airline industry characterized by low profit margins and high fixed costs. The company competes with larger airlines with greater financial resources and faces competition from surface transportation and technological alternatives like virtual meetings. Industry consolidation and the development of low-cost structures by traditional airlines could further impact JetBlue's market position.
  • Operational Risks: The execution of the JetForward strategic plan depends on assumptions about customer demand, fuel costs, and supply chain constraints. Failure to execute this plan could materially impact JetBlue's financial performance. The company is also heavily dependent on fuel availability and price, which are subject to volatility and geopolitical factors. Maintenance costs are expected to rise as the fleet ages, and labor costs may increase as the workforce matures.
  • International Risks: JetBlue's international operations expose it to risks such as compliance with U.S. and foreign regulations, political instability, and economic fluctuations. The company's high aircraft utilization rate makes it vulnerable to delays and cancellations, particularly in the congested New York metropolitan market.
  • Information Security Risks: Significant risks include potential legal claims and reputational harm from data breaches or cyberattacks. Compliance with evolving data security regulations could increase costs and operational complexity.
  • Human Capital Risks: Attracting and retaining qualified personnel in a competitive labor market is challenging. Unionization efforts and labor disputes could increase costs and disrupt operations.
  • Reputational Risks: The potential for accidents or incidents involving JetBlue's aircraft could damage the company's brand and customer trust.
  • Management Strategies: To mitigate these risks, JetBlue employs strategies such as fuel hedging, exploring new market opportunities, maintaining operational efficiency, and focusing on customer satisfaction to strengthen its market position.

SEC Filing: JETBLUE AIRWAYS CORP [ JBLU ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Enbridge reports higher fourth-quarter adjusted profit
data.description:
Pipeline operator Enbridge reported a rise in its adjusted fourth-quarter profit on Friday, on higher Mainline tolls and lower power costs.

The company posted an adjusted profit of C$2.4 billion ($1.69 billion) for the quarter ended December 31, compared with C$2.37 billion a year earlier.

($1 = 1.4185 Canadian dollars)

data.title: Adnoc Drilling Eyes $1 Billion Funding to Refinance Debt, Push Growth
data.description:
Adnoc Drilling (ADX:ADNOCDRILL) will look to secure $1 billion in financing this year to refinance existing debt and support investments and dividends.

The UAE-based drilling company has $750 million of debt set to mature in the fourth quarter, Bloomberg News reported Friday, citing Chief Financial Officer Youssef Salem.

data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea to Continue East Sea Oil, Gas Drilling Despite Initial Setback
data.description:
South Korea's Industry Minister Ahn Duk-geun vowed to push forward with exploratory drilling off the East Sea, rejecting claims that the "Blue Whale" project was a failure, Yonhap News reported Thursday.

Ahn also called reports labeling the findings a failure "inaccurate."

The deep-sea drilling off Yeongil Bay lasted two months from Dec. 20. While initial drilling discovered gas, it fell short of commercial viability. The South Korean government remains committed to further exploration, the report cited Ahn saying.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Above-3% U.S. PCE Could Spark Rate Hike by Fed, BofA Sentiment Survey Shows — Market Talk
data.description:
1209 GMT - U.S. PCE annual inflation of more than 3% could trigger the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates, according to most--or 58%--respondents in Bank of America's February FX and rates sentiment survey. The PCE indicator is the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation. The survey also shows that 5y5y inflation breakeven--a market-based inflation forecast--above 3% would likely be the trigger for a rate hike, according to 12% of respondents. The PCE year-on-year price index was 2.6% in December, while the PCE core index was 2.8% on year. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

1158 GMT - Gold futures rise to near-record levels, though the high price is starting to look overdone, Commerzbank says. Futures are up 0.4% at $2,957.80 a troy ounce, just below the record $2,968.50 an ounce set on Tuesday. The market is reacting asymmetrically to the fresh records, as current developments in the war in Ukraine and lower U.S. interest rate cut expectations--which should be reasonable headwinds--failed to slow gold's rise, Commerzbank's Carsten Fritsch says. President Trump's tariff announcements, however, provided immediate price boost, Fritsch says in a note. Other important influences like the U.S. dollar and bond yields play only minor roles, Fritsch adds. It is difficult to say how long the unusual situation will last. In the short-term, gold could reach $3,000, but this will increase correction potential, Fritsch adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1146 GMT - Bitcoin rises modestly as President Trump's more gradual approach to proposed tariffs provides some respite to risky assets. Trump signed a memo instructing staff to assess retaliatory tariffs on a country by country basis with a plan expected to be released in April. "Investors are expressing relief as Trump's proposed reciprocal tariffs signal a potential opening for negotiations," Tickmill Group's Patrick Munnelly says in a note. Bitcoin is "riding a wave of positivity" with speculators holding a net long position of 786 contracts that bet on the cryptocurrency rising. Bitcoin rises 0.4% to $96,919, according to LSEG. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1134 GMT - Demand for U.K. government bonds has been strong in the first quarter of 2025 despite increased gilt issuance by the Debt Management Office, Tickmill Group's Patrick Munelly says in a note. This has limited any negative impact on gilts, he says. "Overall, the market's appetite has been sufficient to absorb the increased [gilt] supply, mitigating any significant adverse effects on the secondary market," he says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

1133 GMT - Sterling could fall if any potential deal to end the Ukraine-Russia conflict resulted in more aggressive interest-rate cuts from the Bank of England, J.P. Morgan analysts say in a note. Weak U.K. growth already suggests the BOE should accelerate rate cuts but inflation could ease if gas prices decline on any peace deal. If inflation falls, the market could price in more rate cuts, weakening sterling. The prospect of more considerable rate cuts would more than offset the positive impact of improved risk sentiment for sterling. "Furthermore, any increase in regional yields in response to possibility of more defence spending could also be problematic for sterling given its vulnerability given limited fiscal room." (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1128 GMT - The Russian ruble drops after Russia's central bank held interest rates at 21%, as anticipated. The central bank said inflationary pressures remain elevated but should gradually decline in the coming months as lending activity cools and household savings rise. "We think that interest rates are likely to remain on hold until the second half of this year, and that monetary loosening will be more limited than most others expect," Capital Economics economist Nicholas Farr says in a note. If the Ukraine war ended soon, that could increase the scope for rate cuts down the line, he says. USD/RUB rises more than 2% on the day to 91.4504 after the decision, from 90.5001 beforehand. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1107 GMT - The Swedish krona would be one of the biggest beneficiaries of any progress towards a Ukraine ceasefire deal, J.P. Morgan analysts say in a note. The krona's valuation is cheap and it's sensitive to risk sentiment, they say. It's also supported by the potential for Swedish fiscal stimulus and the Riksbank ending its interest-rate cutting cycle. A Ukraine peace deal would provide more relief to the krona compared to the Norwegian krone where the prospect of lower gas prices could impact its terms of trade. J.P. Morgan expects EUR/SEK to fall to 11.00 in the case of a definite resolution. EUR/SEK trades flat at 11.2290, having earlier reached a 20-week low of 11.2112, according to FactSet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

1030 GMT - Eurozone growth of 0.1% is hardly anything for Europe's policymakers to get excited about, capital Economics' Jack-Allen Reynolds says. The eurozone economy expanded by that slight amount in last three months of 2024, according to fresh estimates Friday that bump up a previous estimation of stagnation across the 20 nations that use the euro. That level of growth is still very weak and the signs are for continued sluggishness at the start of 2025, Allen-Reynolds says. "There is also little evidence of a turnaround in the region's woeful productivity performance," he tells investors in a note. (joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby)

0958 GMT - U.S. President Donald Trump has certainly stirred things up with a frenetic stream of policy announcements, but the macro outlook is reasonably settled, says Steven Bell at Columbia Threadneedle Investments in a podcast. "Growth is steady at a goldilocks pace--strong enough to keep the economy well clear of recession but not so strong to push inflation higher," the chief economist says. This week's U.S. CPI data surprised markets with a higher-than-expected print, prompting selloff in U.S. Treasurys. However, Bell says the bigger picture is of a gentle decline in U.S. inflation back towards the Federal Reserve's 2% target. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0955 GMT - Friday's fall in the dollar is unlikely to be sustained as tariff uncertainty remains, MUFG Bank analyst Lee Hardman says in a note. The U.S. currency hits a two-month low against a basket of currencies on relief that President Trump's plans for reciprocal tariffs won't be implemented until at least April. However, reciprocal tariffs will be based not only on tariffs imposed by other countries on U.S. imports but also other factors such as VAT, exchange rates and regulations. "We are more wary of a more disruptive outcome now that non-tariff factors will be included," Hardman says. Trade-policy uncertainty should remain supportive for the dollar, he says. The DXY dollar index falls 0.4% to a two-month low of 106.831. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0954 GMT - The outcome of the Modi-Trump meeting aligns with expectations, with near-term trade frictions set to persist, but a strategic partnership possible over the medium term, according to Nomura analysts. Discussions centered around a trade deal, India increasing energy imports from the U.S., defense cooperation and a reiteration of the strategic partnership, Sonal Varma and Aurodeep Nandi write in a report. Negotiations on a U.S.-India trade deal will begin, with some concessions likely, they say. Talks are expected to focus on India purchasing more U.S. oil & gas, renewable energy and defense equipment, they add. "But given India's higher tariff rate, high non-tariff barriers and its trade surplus with the U.S., it remains in Trump's line of fire on reciprocal tariffs," they write. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

0940 GMT - China's latest credit and lending data suggests policymakers are still struggling to provide much of a lift to broad credit growth, Capital Economics says. China economist Zichun Huang attributes the January headline figures showing new local-currency loans at record highs to the usual seasonal pattern, noting that net lending is always strongest at the start of the year. To cut through the seasonality, CE looks at the data in terms of on-year growth in credit outstanding. On that basis, bank lending slowed from December to a fresh low last month, Huang writes. In the coming quarters, she thinks robust government bond issuance can lend support, but weak private demand will likely keep credit growth subdued. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Kepler Cheuvreux Upgrades Klépierre to Buy, Lifts PT
data.description: Kepler Cheuvreux on Friday upgraded French shopping center operator Klépierre to buy from hold and increased its price target to 35 euros from 31 euros.

data.title: India, US Reportedly Start Talks to Resolve Trade Conflicts
data.description:
The US and India have agreed to start discussions for an early trade deal and to resolve their conflict over tariffs as the latter agreed to buy more US oil, gas and military equipment and fight illegal immigration, multiple news outlets reported Thursday citing a joint statement.

India's Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri said the deal to resolve trade concerns could take place within the next seven months, according to Reuters.

India has agreed to take steps to lower tariffs on certain US products and increase market access to US farm products to negotiate the initial segments of a trade deal by the fall of 2025, the report said.

This comes following talks between US President Donald Trump and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the White House, shortly after Trump unveiled a plan to put reciprocal tariffs on countries that put duties on US imports.

India now wants to double trade with the US by 2030, Modi reportedly said.

data.title: Anchor Securities Downgrades British American Tobacco to Hold, Trims PT
data.description: Anchor Securities Stockbrokers on Friday lowered tobacco products company British American Tobacco (, BTI.JO) to hold from buy and reduced its price target to 34.50 pounds sterling from 35.00 pounds.

data.title: BP’s overhaul can start with a new Chair
data.description:
Activist Elliott Investment Management now has 5% of the $93 bln UK group and wants to sell off its green assets. That might make sense. But investors are more likely to back structural reform if it's overseen by a chair other than current incumbent Helge Lund.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @ywchen1 on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Elliott Investment Management has built a near 5% stake in BP and is pushing the oil company to take radical action to transform its performance, including a big divestment programme, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters on February 13.

Elliott is engaging with the company in advance of its Capital Markets Day, scheduled for February 26, which it sees as a critical event, the person said.

“Building on the actions taken in the last 12 months, we now plan to fundamentally reset our strategy and drive further improvements in performance, all in service of growing cash flow and returns, ” BP CEO Murray Auchincloss said in a statement along with the release of BP’s 2024 results on February 11. “It will be a new direction for BP and we look forward to sharing it at our Capital Markets Update on February 26."

Elliott’s stake in BP was first reported by Bloomberg News on February 8. BP has risen over 6% to 461 pence a share as of 1009 GMT on February 14, since market close on February 7.

data.title: Morgan Stanley CEO Ted Pick's 2024 Compensation Set at $34 Million
data.description:
Morgan Stanley CEO Ted Pick's compensation for 2024 will be $34 million, a company's filing showed on Thursday.

The compensation committee's decision was based on Pick's 'outstanding performance,' including the successful completion of the leadership transition and the firm's exceptional financial performance, the bank said in a filing, citing record revenue, higher earnings, and a market capitalization that surpassed $200 billion.

data.title: Howard Hughes Announces the Opening of Victoria Ward Park Makai and Updated Land Use and Density Guidelines in Ward Village®
data.description:
HONOLULU, Hawaii, Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Ward Village®, an award-winning 60-acre master planned community in the heart of Honolulu, has opened the makai phase of Victoria Ward Park, the community’s first SITES-certified park and the initial phase of a two-part development plan for enhanced public green space. The opening of Victoria Ward Park Makai is part of Ward Village’s broader master plan to create a sustainable, connected community, and coincides with amended HCDA Mauka Area Rules effective January 2025 that establish updated guidelines for smart growth in the district mauka of Ala Moana Boulevard, inclusive of undeveloped Ward Village properties.

The new guidelines represent the potential for an estimated 2.5 - 3.5 million gross square feet of future redevelopment, adding to the community of Ward Village and creating synergies with broader Kaka‘ako. The updated rules emphasize transit-oriented development incentive zones, supporting a balanced mix of homes, public spaces, restaurants, and retail experiences to bring greater public benefit to the neighborhood.

“The new guidelines for smart growth provide the opportunity to deliver much-needed housing to Honolulu, provide a new public park and community gathering place in the island's urban core, and enhance the pristine, walkable community of Ward Village while creating significant value for our shareholders,” said David O’Reilly, Chief Executive Officer of Howard Hughes. “The opening of Victoria Ward Park demonstrates how smart redevelopment can deliver on these goals, and is a great milestone in the continued evolution of Ward Village as the premier community on O’ahu—one that exemplifies our commitment to planning and delivering an exceptional quality of life for all who live and work in our communities.”

Victoria Ward Park Makai is designed to enhance wellness, connectivity and placemaking throughout Ward Village. Recognized as a LEED Neighborhood Development Platinum Certified (LEED-ND) community, Ward Village continues to achieve significant milestones in responsible urban design, including its recent LEED Gold certification for Kō‘ula residences.

“Victoria Ward Park Makai represents the cornerstones of Ward Village’s ongoing vision for the neighborhood,” said Doug Johnstone, President of the Hawaiʻi region for Howard Hughes. “This new public amenity supports our cultivation of an integrated community that seamlessly blends vibrant public spaces, local businesses and housing opportunities for everyone who lives, works and visits Ward Village.”

For more information about Ward Village and its neighborhood upgrades, visit www.wardvillage.com.

About Ward Village®Ward Village is a 60-acre master planned community in the heart of Honolulu, located between downtown and Waikīkī. Honoring the distinct history of its land, Ward Village is at the forefront of sustainable community development—integrating striking architecture, culture and arts, and public open space. At full buildout, the community will include thousands of homes and approximately one million square feet of unique retail in what Architectural Digest named “Best Planned Community in the United States.” As a result of strong demand for new housing in urban Honolulu, Ward Village quickly sold out its first seven mixed-use residential towers—Waiea®, Anaha®, Ae‘o, Ke Kilohana®, ʻAʻaliʻi®, Kō‘ula®, and Victoria Place®—transforming the popular shopping and dining district into a thriving mixed-use neighborhood that offers ocean views and open space, including its newest outdoor gathering place, Victoria Ward Park. New residential buildings in development or under construction include Ulana Ward Village®, The Park Ward Village®, and Kalae. Most recently approved by the Hawai‘i Community Development Authority (HCDA), The Launiu Ward Village will be the latest mixed-use development that will join the neighborhood. Ward Village, a LEED-ND Platinum-certified master planned community part of the Howard Hughes Holdings Inc. portfolio, demonstrates its commitment to responsible, mixed-use development by investing in public benefits and amenities for Honolulu’s urban community. For more information, visit WardVillage.com.

About Howard Hughes Holdings Inc.Howard Hughes Holdings Inc. owns, manages, and develops commercial, residential, and mixed-use real estate throughout the U.S. Its award-winning assets include the country’s preeminent portfolio of master planned communities, as well as operating properties and development opportunities including: Downtown Columbia® in Maryland; The Woodlands®, Bridgeland® and The Woodlands Hills® in the Greater Houston, Texas area; Summerlin® in Las Vegas; Ward Village® in Honolulu, Hawaiʻi; and Teravalis™ in the Greater Phoenix, Arizona area. The Howard Hughes portfolio is strategically positioned to meet and accelerate development based on market demand, resulting in one of the strongest real estate platforms in the country. Dedicated to innovative placemaking, the company is recognized for its ongoing commitment to design excellence and to the cultural life of its communities. Howard Hughes Holdings Inc. is traded on the New York Stock Exchange as HHH. For additional information visit howardhughes.com.

Safe Harbor Statement

Statements made in this press release that are not historical facts, including statements accompanied by words such as “will,” “believe,” “expect,” “enables,” “realize,” “plan,” “intend,” “assume,” “transform” and other words of similar expression, are forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These statements are based on management’s expectations, estimates, assumptions, and projections as of the date of this release and are not guarantees of future performance. Actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied in these statements. Factors that could cause actual results to differ materially are set forth as risk factors in Howard Hughes Holdings Inc.’s filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission, including its Quarterly and Annual Reports. Howard Hughes Holdings Inc. cautions you not to place undue reliance on the forward-looking statements contained in this release. Howard Hughes Holdings Inc. does not undertake any obligation to publicly update or revise any forward-looking statements to reflect future events, information or circumstances that arise after the date of this release.

data.title: TC Energy's Q4 Comparable Earnings Fall, Revenue Rises
data.description:
TC Energy reported Q4 comparable earnings Friday of 1.05 Canadian dollars ($0.74) per common share, down from CA$1.35 a year earlier.

A single analyst polled by FactSet expected CA$1.05.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31. was CA$3.58 billion, up from CA$3.5 billion a year earlier.

Four analysts surveyed by FactSet expected CA$3.66 billion.

TC Energy said it expects 2025 comparable EPS to be lower than 2024 comparable EPS from continuing operations. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect non-GAAP EPS of $3.87.

data.title: DraftKings Reports Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2024 Results
data.description:
DraftKings Inc., a digital sports entertainment and gaming company, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and fiscal year 2024. The company has also raised its 2025 revenue guidance midpoint to $6.45 billion and reaffirmed its 2025 Adjusted EBITDA guidance of $900 million to $1.0 billion.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, DraftKings reported revenue of $1.393 billion, a 13% increase from $1.231 billion in the same period in 2023. The company's net loss for the quarter was $134.9 million, compared to a net loss of $44.6 million in the fourth quarter of 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $89.5 million, down from $151.0 million in the same period last year.

For the full fiscal year 2024, revenue reached $4.768 billion, up from $3.665 billion in 2023. The net loss for the year was $507.3 million, an improvement from a net loss of $802.1 million in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $181.3 million, compared to a negative $151.0 million in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

DraftKings saw a significant increase in Monthly Unique Payers (MUPs), which rose to 4.8 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, a 36% increase from the same period in 2023. Average Revenue per MUP (ARPMUP) was $97, a 16% decrease compared to the fourth quarter of 2023, primarily due to lower ARPMUP for Jackpocket customers and lower actual Sportsbook hold rate.

The company expanded its Sportsbook product offering into new jurisdictions and completed the acquisition of Jackpocket Inc. in May 2024, which contributed to the revenue growth.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

DraftKings achieved its first year of positive Adjusted EBITDA in 2024 and began executing its inaugural share repurchase authorization. The company is live with mobile sports betting in 25 states and Washington, D.C., and with iGaming in 5 states. DraftKings also operates in Ontario, Canada, and expects to launch its Sportsbook product in Missouri and Puerto Rico pending regulatory approvals.

Management's Perspective

Jason Robins, CEO and Co-founder of DraftKings, highlighted the company's efficient customer acquisition and engagement, as well as the expansion of its Sportsbook product. He expressed excitement about enhancing customer economics through new initiatives in live betting and cross-sell efforts. CFO Alan Ellingson noted the strong underlying health of the company's core value drivers and the decision to raise the midpoint of the 2025 revenue guidance.

Future Outlook

DraftKings has raised its fiscal year 2025 revenue guidance to a range of $6.3 billion to $6.6 billion, up from the previous guidance of $6.2 billion to $6.6 billion. The company reaffirmed its fiscal year 2025 Adjusted EBITDA guidance of $900 million to $1.0 billion. The guidance does not include the benefit of year-to-date sport outcomes or the impact of mobile sports betting launching in Missouri.

SEC Filing: DraftKings Inc. [ DKNG ] - 8-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Cooper Standard Reports Improved Operating Income and Positive Cash Flow for Q4 and Full Year 2024
data.description:
Cooper-Standard Holdings Inc., a leading global supplier of sealing and fluid handling systems, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2024. The company reported notable improvements in operating income and positive cash flow despite facing challenges such as lower production volumes and unfavorable foreign exchange rates.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Cooper Standard reported sales of $660.8 million, a slight decrease of 1.9% compared to the same period in 2023. However, the company achieved an operating income of $31.7 million, marking a significant increase of $36.2 million from the previous year. Net income for the quarter was $40.2 million, or $2.24 per diluted share, reflecting an improvement of $95.4 million year-over-year. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $54.3 million, or 8.2% of sales.

For the full year 2024, sales totaled $2.73 billion, a decrease of 3.0% compared to 2023. The company reported an operating income of $69.8 million, up by 51.7% from the previous year. The net loss for the year was $78.7 million, or $(4.48) per diluted share, showing an improvement of $123.2 million compared to 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $180.7 million, or 6.6% of sales.

Business and Operational Highlights

The year-over-year change in fourth quarter sales was primarily due to unfavorable foreign exchange, price adjustments, and lower volume and mix. The improvement in net income was driven by the reversal of certain deferred tax valuation allowances, lower non-cash asset impairment charges, and savings from lean manufacturing and purchasing initiatives. These positive factors were partially offset by higher wages and general inflation, higher net interest expense, and unfavorable foreign exchange.

For the full year, the change in sales was mainly due to unfavorable volume and mix, including price adjustments, the deconsolidation or divestiture of non-core businesses, and unfavorable foreign exchange. The improvement in net loss was driven by the reversal of certain deferred tax valuation allowances, the non-recurrence of refinancing and debt extinguishment expense, and savings from lean manufacturing and purchasing initiatives.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Cooper Standard continues to leverage its engineering and manufacturing capabilities to win new business awards, particularly in the electric vehicle segment. For the full year 2024, the company received net new business awards representing $181.4 million in incremental anticipated future annualized sales, including $105.8 million on electric vehicle platforms.

Management's Perspective

Jeffrey Edwards, Chairman and CEO of Cooper Standard, commented, "We were able to deliver profit, cash flow, and margin improvement essentially in line with our original guidance and expectations, despite lower production and foreign exchange headwinds. The new organizational structure we implemented at the beginning of 2024 continues to drive significant efficiencies and cost savings, and we expect to continue the momentum of operational excellence and margin enhancement in 2025."

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, Cooper Standard expects to continue driving operating efficiencies to offset production volume and inflation headwinds. The company has provided initial guidance for 2025, with sales projected to be between $2.7 billion and $2.8 billion, and adjusted EBITDA expected to range from $200 million to $235 million.

SEC Filing: Cooper-Standard Holdings Inc. [ CPS ] - 8-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: AMC Networks Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
AMC Networks Inc. has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2024. The company, known for its popular TV series and films, has provided a detailed overview of its financial performance, operational highlights, and strategic initiatives.

Financial Highlights

For the full year 2024, AMC Networks reported net revenues of $2.42 billion, a decrease of 10.7% compared to the previous year. The company experienced an operating loss of $40 million, which included significant impairment and restructuring charges. Adjusted Operating Income (AOI) was $563 million, with a margin of 23%. The diluted earnings per share (EPS) was $(5.10), while the adjusted EPS was $3.86.

In the fourth quarter, net revenues were $599 million, down 11.7% from the same period in 2023. The operating loss for the quarter was $254 million, with an AOI of $129 million and a margin of 22%. The diluted EPS for the quarter was $(6.38), and the adjusted EPS was $0.64.

Business and Operational Highlights

AMC Networks achieved several operational milestones in 2024. The company completed significant affiliate renewal activities, representing almost half of its domestic affiliate subscriber base. It also signed a multi-year renewal with Amazon Prime Video Channels for global distribution of its streaming services. The company expanded its FAST channels business to 19 live channels across 12 platforms, and launched AMCN Outcomes, a new performance product for advertisers.

AMC Networks continued to grow its streaming revenues, which increased by 7% to $603 million, driven by an 8% increase in streaming subscribers to 12.4 million. The company also saw a 41% increase in international advertising revenues, primarily due to one-time retroactive adjustments and strong performance in the UK and European markets.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

In 2024, AMC Networks focused on strategic programming assessments and restructuring. The company recorded a $268.7 million goodwill impairment charge in its Domestic Operations unit and $102 million in its International unit. Additionally, the company did not repurchase any shares under its stock repurchase program, which has $135 million of authorization remaining.

AMC Networks also issued new senior secured notes and convertible senior notes, while redeeming and repurchasing existing notes to manage its debt profile. The company reported a net cash provided by operating activities of $376 million and free cash flow of $331 million for the year.

Management's Perspective

Kristin Dolan, CEO of AMC Networks, expressed satisfaction with the company's performance, highlighting the achievement of full-year guidance across key financial metrics and strong free cash flow generation. Dolan emphasized the importance of innovative partnerships and high-quality content in driving the company's success amidst industry challenges.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead, AMC Networks has increased its free cash flow expectations to approximately $550 million over the 2024-2025 period. The company plans to continue delivering high-quality content and expanding its advertising capabilities. The 2025 programming slate includes new series such as "Anne Rice’s The Talamasca" and "Nautilus," along with returning favorites like "The Walking Dead" franchise.

SEC Filing: AMC Networks Inc. [ AMCX ] - 8-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Earnings Flash (ENB) Enbridge Posts Q4 Adjusted EPS of CA$0.75, vs. FactSet Est of CA$0.75
Netmarble Narrows Loss in Q4
data.description:
Netmarble posted a net loss attributable to shareholders of 162.7 billion won in the fourth quarter of 2024, narrowing from a net attributable loss of 171.9 billion won a year earlier.

Sales of the South Korean game developer fell 2.5% year over year to 649 billion won from 665.6 billion won, according to a Thursday Korea Exchange filing.

Visible Alpha estimated sales of 678.2 billion won in Q4.

Shares of Netmarble fell 1% at market close on Friday.

data.title: NFI Brief: New York's MTA Further Electrifies Fleet, With Order For 265 Battery-Electric Buses From NFI Subsidiary
Cloudbreak Discovery Resumes Trading on London Bourse
data.description:
Cloudbreak Discovery's shares recommenced trading on the London bourse's main market at 7:30 am local time on Friday.

The royalty company said the trading suspension, which started on Nov. 1, 2024, was lifted after it published its annual report for the year ended June 30, 2024.

ACCO Brands Corporation Declares Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
ACCO Brands Corporation Declares Quarterly Dividend

LAKE ZURICH, Ill.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--February 14, 2025--

ACCO Brands Corporation today announced that its board of directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.075 per share. The dividend will be paid on March 26, 2025 to stockholders of record as of the close of business on March 14, 2025.

"This is the Company's 29(th) quarterly cash dividend since it began paying dividends in 2018. The Company's dividend has become an important part of our capital allocation strategy, and we remain committed to supporting our quarterly dividend with our robust free cash flow. At the current stock price, on an annualized basis, our shareholders are receiving an approximate 6% yield on their investment," said Tom Tedford, President, and Chief Executive Officer of ACCO Brands.

About ACCO Brands Corporation

ACCO Brands, the Home of Great Brands Built by Great People, designs, manufactures and markets consumer and end-user products that help people work, learn and play. Our widely recognized brands include AT-A-GLANCE(R), Five Star(R), Kensington(R), Leitz(R), Mead(R), PowerA(R), Swingline(R), Tilibra(R) and many others. More information about ACCO Brands Corporation can be found at www.accobrands.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250214664151/en/

data.title: Stocks Down Pre-Bell Ahead of Latest Retail Sales Report; Asia, Europe Mostly Up
data.description:
US equity futures were pointing lower on Friday as traders digest inflation data from earlier in the week and await the retail sales report for January.

Standard & Poor's 500 and the Nasdaq declined 0.2% each in premarket activity while the Dow Jones Industrial Average was off 0.4%. Most Asian and European exchanges were trending upwards.

The Bureau of Labor Statistics on Thursday reported that US producer price growth rose more than Wall Street's expectations last month. On Wednesday, government data showed that consumer inflation accelerated in January on both sequential and annual bases.

On Friday's economic calendar, the retail sales data and the import and export prices report, both for last month, are out at 8:30 am ET. The industrial production bulletin for January posts at 9:15 am, followed by business inventories report for December at 10 am.

The weekly Baker Hughes domestic oil-and-gas rig count is scheduled for a 1 pm release. Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas President Lorie Logan is slated to speak at 3 pm.

Shares of Applied Materials dropped 5.1% pre-bell as the semiconductor equipment manufacturer issued a downbeat fiscal second-quarter revenue outlook at the midpoint. Airbnb jumped 13% as the vacation rental company reported a bigger-than-expected fourth-quarter profit. Palo Alto Networks declined 5.7% following its quarterly results.

Enbridge , Fortis , Moderna , Magna International , Essent Group , Portland General Electric , Newmark Group and Treehouse Foods report their latest financial results early Friday, among others.

Before the opening bell, bitcoin inclined 0.7% to $96,830, West Texas Intermediate crude oil slipped 0.1% to $71.24 per barrel, yields on 10-year Treasuries nudged 0.8 basis points higher to 4.53% and gold rose 0.4% to $2,958 an ounce.
